pages.clean_text:
Largest Public Health Data Platform
Stopping Disease Through Public Health Intelligence
For over a decade, we’ve delivered disease surveillance and syndromic solutions protecting millions of Americans. We’re proud to share that
SSDataInfo
and
Envision Technology
have joined
InductiveHealth
, strengthening our expertise across public health systems—including IIS through WebIZ.
CONNECT WITH AN EXPERT
Trusted Partners
The Integrated Advantage
Proven Community Protection
At Scale When It Matters Most
When COVID-19 hit, public health agencies faced unprecedented challenges: onboard hundreds of new testing laboratories, process millions of test results, coordinate vaccination efforts, and monitor syndromic signals all simultaneously, all under emergency conditions.  Every point of COVID data you saw, we touched.
The integrated infrastructure that accelerated pandemic response now enables faster action across all public health threats from measles outbreaks, to wastewater, to foodborne illness investigations.
https://www.inductivehealth.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Inductive-Health-90.mp4
Disease Surveillance - Syndromic Surveillance - Immunization Information
One Platform for Complete Public Health Readiness
Public health agencies have long managed disease surveillance, syndromic monitoring, and immunization systems separately. We bring them together enabling epidemiologist to investigate cases across all data streams, detect threats earlier, and protect communities faster. Build on a decade of CDC -standard implementation across 40+ state, local and territorial jurisdictions.
A single integrated surveillance architecture through one vendor, all at a lower total cost of ownership
Unify electronic disease surveillance systems, ESSENCE® syndromic monitoring, and immunization registries into a single platform. Investigate cases across multiple data streams without switching systems, while eliminating duplicate vendor relationships and redundant data entry. HL7 messaging, ELR, and eCR data flow through standardized interfaces, giving your epidemiology team the complete picture to detect, investigate, and respond to threats faster.
Respond to outbreaks faster with standard-based disease surveillance
Manage notifiable disease investigations through our cloud-native InductiveHealth EDSS, CDC-developed NEDSS Base System (NBS), or open-source EpiTrax. Automate ELR ingestion, eCR processing, and MMG case notification to CDC, reducing manual data entry, allowing epidemiologist to focus on investigation rather than paperwork. Browser-based access means your public health team investigates cases from anywhere with secure authentication.
See Capabilities
Identify illness patterns and emergencies earlier through real-time syndromic surveillance
Detect threats before laboratory confirmation by analyzing emergency department visits, urgent car encounters, and pharmacy data. Configure surveillance zones for your jurisdiction, monitor ILI and respiratory syndromes, and

pages.title: About Us - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
About us
Learn more about  InductiveHealth
connect with an expert
What do we do
Leader in public health disease surveillance
Since our founding in 2013, InductiveHealth has become a leading provider in disease surveillance systems throughout the United States. Inductivehealth partners with Federal, State, Tribal, Local, and Territorial jurisdictions to deliver unique and scalable disease surveillance solutions to fit our clients needs and the ever changing public health landscape.
Our vision and values
Our vision is for our technology to positively impact over 1 million lives per year. Everyday, we are striving to accomplish this goal by living our values to the fullest:
Think Differently
See the upside
Earn trust
Act with integrity
Driven to succeed
Our people
Work with experts in public health
The InductiveHealth team consists of experienced epidemiologists and public health experts as well as trained engineers and technologists who all share a common goal: improve the public health industry through technology.

pages.title: InductiveHealth EDSS - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
Our Latest Disease Surveillance Technology
InductiveHealth EDSS
The most scalable disease surveillance and general public health platform on the market, powered by Origami Risk.
Cloud-native technology that eliminates infrastructure burden, accelerates implementation, and gives epidemiologists the autonomy to adapt system configuration and reduce vendor dependency.
Electronic Disease Surveillance Built On Proven Government Risk Technology Infrastructure
Enterprise-Grade Security
Origami Risk’s layered security framework with role-based access, comprehensive audit logging, and MFA ensures HIPAA and FedRAMP compliance while protecting sensitive health data.
Autonomous Configuration
Self-service admin tools enable epidemiologists to modify workflows, add custom fields, and deploy new forms without IT dependencies or vendor customization requests.
Seamless Interoperability
Secure API integrations, HL7 messaging, and real-time data exchange with laboratories, EHRs, and other healthcare systems eliminate manual data handling.
Turn-Key Disease Surveillance That's Built Differently
Cloud-native architecture that minimizes overhead
True multi-tenant SaaS on a scalable foundation, eliminates server procurement, database administration, and IT maintenance. Handle surge events without intervention, supported by the highest SLAs in the industry and backed by AWS and Azure enterprise infrastructure.
Access from anywhere. Investigate cases from any device.
Built-in disaster recovery. Automated backups and disaster recovery capabilities ensure continuity during system disruptions.
More control for users with self-service configuration and improved team coordination
A robust visual admin interface puts configuration control directly in epidemiologists’ hands. Create custom fields, modify workflows, and deploy new investigation forms in hours, and without the need for vendor development cycles or IT support.
Coordinated task management, shared case access, and integrated email communication enable seamless team collaboration across investigation workflows with role-based access control
One system for data collection to decision-making
Built-in analytics eliminate dependency on external BI tools or separate reporting databases. Epidemiologists configure dashboards with out-of-box available modules, including state-specific geo visualizations, heat maps, trend analysis, case lists, and task queues, all customizable for specific roles and surveillance properties.
Actionable, real-time data. Query live case management data, not yesterday’s data warehouse extract. Changes to case status, laboratory results, or outbreak associations appear instantly in dashboards.
Stratification at scale. Pre-configured views for STI programs, respiratory illness surveillance, ECR processing, and outbreak management. Create additional custom stratifications with minimal effort.
Reliable support infrastructure that’s ready for 24/7 outbreak response
Providing the fastest implementation t

headings: Turn-Key Disease Surveillance That's Built Differently
One system for data collection to decision-making
Reliable support infrastructure that’s ready for 24/7 outbreak response

pages.title: EpiTrax - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
Solutions
EpiTrax™
A modern system for the most efficient, configurable, electronic disease surveillance solution provided in a commercial, open-source model that is NEDSS-compatible and meets CDC standards.
A Highly Configurable Platform & Experienced Provider
Open Source Technology
Highly configurable for integrations and custom requirements, and reduces the risk of vendor lock-in.
The Only Way To Use EMSA
Ensure greater visibility and control over incoming electronic messages with an easy-to-use, fully configured Electronic Message Staging Area user interface.
Innovative Technology
Get up and running quickly with hosting support, AI-enhanced data migration services from any system, and initial configuration and setup.
Modern & Integrated Disease Surveillance
More than traditional Electronic Disease Surveillance Systems
Increase efficiencies for public health teams with better data entry and more features, such as outbreak management, contact tracing, case management, update forms without coding, and other new capabilities that traditional systems do not provide.
Streamlined team & case management
Dashboards make it easy to manage cases through tasks and assignments with easy prioritization using condition, due dates, geographies, investigators, or other filters. Workflows enable collaboration and case flows across public health nurses, case investigators, and epidemiology teams.
Confidential morbidity report (CMR) display
Easily associate comorbidities with a condition, and include previous instances of the same condition or related conditions.
Configurable beyond standard investigations
Supplemental forms are configurable across various populations, for scenarios such as follow-up interview protocols, drug treatment regimens, or contact tracing.

headings: A Highly Configurable Platform & Experienced Provider
The Only Way To Use EMSA
More than traditional Electronic Disease Surveillance Systems

pages.title: NBS - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
Solutions
NBS
InductiveHealth is the premier implementer of the NEDSS Base System (NBS) with more than a dozen successful jurisdictional implementations.  For over 10 years, we have provided deployment, upgrades, support, and hosting of the NBS for state and territorial public health agencies.
The Government-Supported Standard of EDSS
Decades of NBS Expertise
Over a decade of NBS support and a key technology provider during the COVID-19 pandemic supporting efficient ELR processing, data migrations, and Page Builder expertise.
NBS 7.0 & Data Modernization
Partnered with the CDC to prepare for a future NBS vision and readying state jurisdictions for the upcoming version 7.0 upgrade to provide a seamless transition from legacy environments.
The CDC Standard Solution
Ensuring departments are sending timely and accurate data to CDC via the latest notification standards including GenV2, condition-specific MMGs, NETSS, NEDSS, etc., while providing easier integration with AIMS Hub and other government reporing tools.
The Most Trusted Partner for NBS Solutions & Support
Supporting More NBS Implementations Than Any Other Provider
Our track record supporting NBS implementations spans more than a decade and more than two dozen NBS implementations.  We test each patch and release, using a proprietary upgrade path that mitigates performance issues and data loss.
The Foundation for Processing Electronic Data Ingestion
In addition to being the key tool in disease surveillance and case management, NBS is also foundational for ingesting and processing electronic lab reports (ELR) and electronic case reports (eCR).  We are experts in supporting these activities through our knowledge of Page Builder for form configuration, the database schema for data porting and ad-hoc reporting, and configuration of all systems to properly ingest and route electronic data to program staff.
Expertise in Key Solutions Built to Enhance NBS
We bring a a deep experience in providing expanded SaaS services, including support of Rhapsody, FHIR, and Mirth.  Activities include resolving errors in integration engines, custom coding for ELR and eCR using both standard and non-standard reporting methods, developing custom Rhapsody routes and specialized processing, and integrating SQL and T-SQL application database structure.
Integration With Syndromic Data
Jurisdictions can unlock further surveillance capabilities by leveraging a proprietary approach that ties syndromic and disease surveillance together within a single Integrated Disease Surveillance System platform with custom NBS and ESSENCE capabilities.

headings: The Most Trusted Partner for NBS Solutions & Support
Supporting More NBS Implementations Than Any Other Provider
The Foundation for Processing Electronic Data Ingestion
Expertise in Key Solutions Built to Enhance NBS

pages.title: WebIZ™ - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
Immunization Information Systems
Envision WebIZ™
Now part of InductiveHealth’s unified public health platform, WebIZ™ brings together 20+ years of immunization registry excellence with leading disease and syndromic surveillance capabilities.
As the nation’s most widely deployed immunization information system provider for state and local government public health agencies, and largest contributor to CDC’s IZGateway, WebIZ™ is designed to meet CDC standards for effective tracking and administration of immunizations across any jurisdiction.
Designed for Every Level of Immunization Management
Vaccine Program Operations
Keep vaccines safe, available, and properly managed with real-time inventory tracking, VTrckS automated federal ordering, and temperature monitoring with configurable alerts.
Immunization Registry
Document, track, and share lifetime immunization records via CDC clinical decision support, bidirectional HL7 integration with EHRs, and IZ Gateway for nationwide data exchange.
Coverage Assessment & Compliance
Measure population immunity and ensure regulatory compliance with embedded analytics dashboards, school certificate compliance, and automated federal reporting.
One Platform For All Immunization Programs
Infrastructure that scales from routine clinics to mass vaccinations
Built on Microsoft .NET and SQL Server with flexible deployment options on Azure Government Cloud for scalable managed hosting. Browser-based access from anywhere, whether it’s a clinic, mobile van, or school nurse laptop.
Highly configurable to meet jurisdiction-specific immunization requirements
Commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) platform with configurable modules for standard and special programs management, including perinatal Hepatitis B, VFC/317 coordination, and state-specific requirements.
WebIZ provides admin tools that put control in public health hands, allowing users to toggle system parameters such as adding new vaccine products through code tables when CDC approves them, or updating school certificate rules when state law changes.
Robust Analytics that can identify under-vaccinated populations before outbreaks happen
Embedded Power BI dashboards show coverage rates by ZIP code, vaccine type, age group, and provider, with no custom development required. Public health officials target interventions to specific geographic and demographic gaps, while clinic managers identify patients due this week.
Near real-time visibility with rapid dashboard updates. Vaccines administered through HL7-integrated providers appear in analytics quickly, minimizing delays for coverage assessment and outbreak response.
Live operational data and preset filters to quickly access trends and patient records. Find age groups due for particular vaccines in just a few clicks.
Community Development Model That Accelerates Immunization Program Improvements
Over 20 years serving 20+ jurisdictions means proven implementation patterns for VFC workflows, perinatal Hepatitis B tracking, and comp

headings: Designed for Every Level of Immunization Management
One Platform For All Immunization Programs
Infrastructure that scales from routine clinics to mass vaccinations
Highly configurable to meet jurisdiction-specific immunization requirements
Community Development Model That Accelerates Immunization Program Improvements

pages.title: ESSENCE - Inductive
pages.clean_text:
Solutions
Syndromic Surveillance powered by ESSENCE®
In 2021, InductiveHealth became the sole commercial provider of Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory’s premier health surveillance innovation known as ESSENCE. Electronic Surveillance System for the Early Notification of Community-based Epidemics (ESSENCE) is the leading solution for capturing and analyzing health-indicator data to identify abnormal health conditions and enable early detection and monitoring of events of public health importance.
InductiveHealth for Syndromic Surveillance
Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory Relationship
In 2021, InductiveHealth became the sole commercial provider of Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory’s premier health surveillance innovation known as ESSENCE.
Syndromic Surveillance Expertise
Work with our experienced public health staff who combined have more than 30+ years of previous ESSENCE and syndromic surveillance experience. Our staff will work to understand your goals and customize ESSENCE to fit those needs.
Access Timely Data
Access InductiveHealth’s experienced team of Syndromic Surveillance subject matter experts to stay up-to-date with the latest data and identify unusual illness patterns giving you the ability to respond swiftly to public health emergencies.
Enhanced Syndromic Surveillance
Integrate With Electronic Disease Surveillance
With an InductiveHealth’s integrated disease surveillance system (IDSS) implementation, unlock enhanced capabilities by integrating with disease surveillance platforms such as NBS or EpiTrax™ to cross-reference datasets, and access both systems from a single secure environment with real-time multi-user collaboration fortified with MFA and HIPAA-compliant standards.
System Flexibility to Control Your Data
A local instance of ESSENCE allows jurisdictions to scale and tailor ESSENCE offerings based on the jurisdiction’s needs, control what data feeds are onboarded, and integrate additional data sources.
More Syndromic Data Integration and Analysis
Ingest additional data sets not available with other solutions, including: ED, urgent care, inpatient, hospital discharge, air quality, weather, death, reportable conditions, school absentee, EMS, poison control, OTC drug sales, ASPR, DMAT, lab, hospital bed availability and more.
Customized and Targeted Surveillance
Enable more accurate and targeted surveillance crucial for localized health monitoring and response by defining geographies to the district and neighborhood level. Expand tracked geographies across jurisdiction borders and automate alerts for custom geographies to pinpoint areas and populations that require immediate attention.
InductiveHealth Success Stories
How ESSENCE® and Data Visualization Help Houston Track Extreme Heat Related Illnesses
Learn More
